cot_prefix: Okay, I'm going to try to figure out the best title for this passage. Let me read through the text again to make sure I understand the main points. The passage starts by saying that if you want to see the effects of global warming, you should head north, and Will Steger is taking people there. Steger is an experienced polar explorer and environmentalist who's been raising awareness about global warming. He's leading a team on a dogsled trip across Ellesmere Island in the Canadian Arctic to document the changes. They're uploading content to a website so others can follow along. The article mentions specific team members and highlights that the Arctic's geography is changing due to climate change. It also states that within a decade, reaching the North Pole by dog team might require flotation due to melting ice. The conclusion is that while climate change is happening, people can still change their actions to affect the Earth's future. Now, looking at the options: (A) The Arctic Is In Danger (B) Discoveries of the Arctic (C) Sledding Through the Arctic (D) Consequences of Global Warming Option A focuses on the Arctic being in danger. The passage does talk about the Arctic's changing geography and the effects of global warming there, so this is a possibility. Option B is about discoveries. The passage is more about showing the effects of global warming rather than new discoveries, so maybe not the best fit. Option C mentions sledding through the Arctic. The story is about Steger's dogsled trip,
